title: Audit first Sonnet poller run + empower /tmp clone cleanup on disk pressure
scope_authority: tinker is empowered to take corrective action DIRECTLY where described — not just file a follow-up PROP. See per-task authority lines.
context: Poller flipped from Opus 4.7 to Sonnet 4.6 on 2026-05-07 between the 11:06Z and 23:06Z scheduled runs. The 23:08Z run was the first Sonnet pass.

Three other agent demotions (workspace-sync, integrity, social) all held format and substance on first Sonnet runs. Poller is the load-bearing test because its last_poll_note is consumed by analyst / decider / curmudgeon / integrity on every cycle.

Operator preliminary read of 23:08Z run: chg-* artifacts (3 files) look substantively correct — HIGH-band transition (Step 10b day-8+) for 8 EOM PREDs, 20-day manual-commit drought milestone, 10-commit automated rollup. No obvious factual errors.

BUT: monitor/status.json's last_poll field still reads 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z (the prior Opus run), and last_poll_note text is the 11:06Z narrative. Sonnet wrote the chg-* files but appears to have skipped updating these canonical pipeline-state fields. Downstream readers will see a stale poller signal.

tasks.description: Read monitor/changes/chg-20260507-2308-001/002/003.json AND monitor/status.json. Compare last_poll / last_poll_note / changes_pending_analysis / consecutive_quiet_polls fields against the chg-* file timestamps and content. Verify whether Sonnet's run produced ALL canonical artifacts the prompt asks for.
specifically_check: Did last_poll_note get rewritten with the new poll's narrative (HIGH-band transition + 20-day drought + auto rollup)?
Did changes_pending_analysis increment correctly (the 23:08Z run produced 1 substantive chg-*; consistent accounting convention)?
Did consecutive_quiet_polls reset to 0 (substantive HIGH-band-transition chg-* should reset)?

Did the chg-* file structure match prior Opus runs (severity, kind/type fields populated, schema-conformant)? Operator quick-check showed severity=None, kind=None on all three files via terse JSON inspection — verify whether that's a schema regression or just operator-side reading.

Compare 23:08Z chg-* narrative density against a sample of prior Opus chg-* files (e.g., chg-20260506-2306-001). Sonnet may produce slightly terser narratives — is the substance preserved (KEY FINDINGs, watch-for-next-poll items)?

tasks.description: If task 1 finds the canonical state-update fields stale (last_poll, last_poll_note, etc.), TAKE CORRECTIVE ACTION DIRECTLY — do not file a PROP and wait for human review. Two acceptable mechanisms:

mechanism_a_backfill_status_json.when: Sonnet's run produced correct chg-* artifacts but skipped status.json field updates. Backfill is mechanical.
mechanism_a_backfill_status_json.what: Synthesize the last_poll_note from the 23:08Z chg-* files content. Update last_poll, last_poll_note, changes_pending_analysis, consecutive_quiet_polls in monitor/status.json (FUSE workspace). Workspace-sync rounds it to git within 1h.
mechanism_a_backfill_status_json.preserve: Other-owned fields in status.json (last_decider_*, last_analyst_*, etc.) MUST stay untouched — only update poller-owned fields per the file's existing schema.

mechanism_b_revert_poller_to_opus.when: Sonnet's run produced material content errors (e.g., factual error in a chg-* file, missing a real signal, wrong severity classification, narrative format that breaks analyst's parsing) that affect downstream pipeline correctness.
mechanism_b_revert_poller_to_opus.what: File HNOTE-OPERATOR-POLLER-REVERT-001 in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json explaining what regressed and recommending the operator revert poller's model to Opus 4.7 (or Opus 4.6 as middle rung). Specific evidence cited (chg-* file paths, what went wrong). HNOTE is operator-actionable; tinker does NOT change the scheduled-task model directly.
mechanism_b_revert_poller_to_opus.rationale: Model selection is operator-controlled in the Cowork UI — tinker can't flip it. So the corrective lever for content-quality regressions is an HNOTE asking the operator.

tasks.authority: WRITE empowered for mechanism A (backfill status.json poller-owned fields). WRITE empowered for mechanism B (file HNOTE). Either is single-commit, FUSE-side, no schema additions.

tasks.description: Standing empowerment, not just this run: when tinker's Mode 2 disk audit detects /tmp footprint > 500MB AND any /tmp/*-clone directory older than 24h, tinker is empowered to `rm -rf` the stale clones DIRECTLY. Log the cleanup in the tinker run report (cleaned_paths array). Also describe the policy briefly in monitor/prompts/tinker.md so future tinker runs know it's authorized.
tasks.rationale_with_evidence: Disk-fill recurrence killed decider's clone path overnight 2026-05-07→08. Bash sandbox went to 100% on root, decider fell into degraded-fuse mode, applied EXP-293 archive-cite patch via file tools without JSON-validity checks, broke wins.json, took ~3h to detect and recover. Three /tmp/*-clone directories (prop017-clone 33h old, check-clone, check2) accounted for 600MB+ of the 940MB project footprint tinker reported. Cleanup is mechanical, low-risk (operator-created clones can always be re-cloned in <30s), and removes the structural cause.
safety_constraints: ONLY remove directories matching the glob /tmp/*-clone (or /tmp/*-clone-*).
REQUIRE mtime older than 24h — never touch a fresh active clone.
Log every removal in the tinker run report under findings.cleanup_actions[].
If unsure about a directory, file a HNOTE-OPERATOR-DISK-CLEANUP-AMBIGUOUS-NNN listing what tinker considered but didn't clean, and let the operator decide.
exclusions: Never touch /tmp/edit-clone if its mtime is fresh (<2h) — the operator may be actively using it for a session.
Never touch /sessions/intelligent-brave-lamport/ or any FUSE mount.
tasks.authority: WRITE empowered to `rm -rf` matching directories AND to add the policy paragraph to monitor/prompts/tinker.md. Single-commit acceptable.

completion_criteria: (1) task 1 audit results are in tinker's next run report. (2) IF a regression was found in task 1, mechanism A or B was taken (not just proposed). (3) tinker.md has the disk-cleanup policy paragraph (one-time addition). (4) directive status flips pending → completed with `completed_at` and `completed_by_run` per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE.
cross_references: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-024-workspace-sync-json-validity-gate.json (overnight wins.json incident — also on tinker's plate)
monitor/decisions/applied-patches/applied-2026-05-08T06-10-00Z-degraded-fuse.json (the degraded-fuse EXP-293 patch that broke wins.json — root cause was disk pressure)
